subheading_main.en: An ecosystem to build custom-made translation tools

intro.en: Pankosmia is a scripture translation software Ecosystem. It is used to build multiple translation tools, using shared components.

philosophy_intro_paragraph.en: Pankosmia began with a conversation about the [First the monkey](https://blog.x.company/tackle-the-monkey-first-90fd6223e04d) principle. TLDR, if your project is to have a monkey recite Shakespeare on a pedestal, don't focus on the pedestal. In other words, tackle the hard bit first. For us, two of the hard bits were graphite support and guaranteeing an offline-first experience where failing to do so may put people in danger.

philosophy_corollaries.en: Some corollaries of "First the Monkey" for us are:

philosophy_corollary1.en: focus on the hard and unsolved problems

philosophy_corollary2.en: the working but clunky solution we can build and ship is better than both the lovely solution we can't build and ship and the lovely UI we can ship that doesn't do the right thing under the hood

philosophy_corollary3.en: polishing things that we know how to polish is rarely urgent

philosophy_corollary4.en: users need happy devs because devs build the systems the users need

distinctive1.en: open source (but third-party products may include closed-source clients)

distinctive4.en: built around Scripture Burrito for all content

distinctive5.en: multiple "white app" products that can be branded and maintained by different organizations and developed to meet different user needs

distinctive6.en: multiple clients per product, including standard clients for core functionality

architecture_paragraph.en: Products ship as an Electronite app, ie Electron with added support for graphite-enabled fonts. A product typically includes:

architecture1.en: a localhost HTTP server, written in Rust, that handles

architecture1c.en: access to burrito servers (when Internet is enabled)

architecture2.en: multiple clients, served as built HTML/CSS/Javascript:

architecture2a.en: core clients use React and MUI, use of other frameworks is possible

architecture3.en: core Node libraries to simplify the creation of new clients

architecture4.en: an extensive online build toolchain for multiple operating systems
